name: LattePanda Mu (compute module + carrier kit)
description: Intel N100 module + carrier (treated as a usable SBC kit)

manufacturer.comment: LattePanda is the brand; the company behind it is DFRobot / Zhiwei Robotics Corp. (Shanghai).

status.comment: DFRobot still lists module ($159) and evaluation kit ($209) as in-stock new (checked 2026-04-26).

open-source-hardware.comment: The Lite and Full Eval carrier boards are released as open-source KiCAD designs under the repo's MIT license. The compute module's own PCB source files are NOT published — only pinouts, symbols, and footprint libraries. Per RESEARCH.md ("Schematics-only does not qualify"), the kit as a whole is marked false; carrier boards are openly designed and reproducible.

country-of-origin.comment: DFRobot / Zhiwei Robotics is headquartered in Shanghai, China.

soc.comment: Base/most-common config is N100; an N305 (Core i3-N305, 8 cores) variant is also sold. SoC value reflects the base config; max-clock attribute uses N305 stack (see comment there).

cpu-cores.comment: Base config N100 has 4 cores / 4 threads. The N305 SKU has 8 cores; see ram-max / msrp-top for the upper-tier config.

cpu-architecture.value: Intel N100 (Alder Lake-N, 4 cores / 4 threads, Gracemont E-cores)

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: N100 max turbo per Intel ARK is 3.40 GHz. The N305 SKU on the same module reaches 3.8 GHz.

process-node.comment: Alder Lake-N is fabricated on Intel 7.

gpu.comment: Xe-LP-derived UHD Graphics, 24 EUs at up to 750 MHz on N100; 32 EUs at up to 1.25 GHz on the N305 SKU.

opengl-es-version.comment: Gen12 Intel graphics expose OpenGL ES 3.2 via Mesa on Linux; OpenGL 4.6 also supported.

vulkan-support.comment: Intel Gen12 (Alder Lake-N UHD) is Vulkan 1.x conformant via mainline Mesa anv/vulkan-intel.

npu.comment: Alder Lake-N predates Intel's NPU (introduced with Meteor Lake).

hw-video-decode.comment: Intel Quick Sync Video on Alder Lake-N supports H.264, H.265 (HEVC), VP9, and AV1 hardware decode.

hw-video-encode.comment: Quick Sync on Alder Lake-N encodes H.264 and HEVC. AV1 hardware encode arrived with Arc / Meteor Lake; not present on N100. VP9 encode is also not supported.

ram-min-gb.comment: Smallest configuration sold is 8GB LPDDR5 (N100 SKU).

ram-max-gb.comment: Top configuration is 16GB LPDDR5 4800 MT/s (N305 SKU).

emmc.comment: 64 GB onboard eMMC 5.1 (HS400) on the module.

microsd-slot.comment: Neither the module nor the standard Lite Carrier expose a microSD slot.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: Lite Carrier exposes an M.2 M-key (2230) slot for NVMe SSD via PCIe 3.0 x1; the Full Eval Carrier adds an additional PCIe 3.0 x4 slot.

sata.comment: The module supports up to 2× SATA 6 Gbps natively, but neither the Lite Carrier nor the Full Eval Carrier expose a SATA port; SATA is only available on user-designed carriers.

ethernet.comment: Lite Carrier ships with a single 1 GbE port (Realtek RTL8111). The Full Eval Carrier provides 2× 2.5 GbE — see dual-ethernet comment.

dual-ethernet.comment: Lite Carrier (the standard kit) has only one Ethernet port. The optional Full Eval Carrier offers dual 2.5 GbE.

wifi.comment: No Wi-Fi shipped; M.2 E-key 2230 slot accepts a user-supplied Wi-Fi/Bluetooth card.

bluetooth.comment: No Bluetooth shipped; depends on the user's M.2 E-key add-in card.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: Lite Carrier exposes 2× USB 3.2 Gen 2 (10 Gbps) Type-A.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: The Lite Carrier's single USB-C port is power-only (15V PD input) — not a data port.

poe-support.comment: No PoE on either the Lite Carrier or the Full Eval Carrier.

hdmi-outputs.comment: Lite Carrier has a single HDMI 2.0 output. The module itself can drive up to 3 HDMI/DP outputs simultaneously, and the Full Eval Carrier breaks out 2× HDMI.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.0 on the Lite Carrier; module supports up to 4096×2160 @ 60 Hz on each display interface.

displayport.comment: No native DisplayPort on the Lite Carrier (only HDMI 2.0); the module exposes DP 1.4 lanes that custom carriers can break out.

mipi-dsi.comment: Lite Carrier has no MIPI-DSI connector. The module provides eDP 1.4 (not DSI) for embedded panels.

mipi-csi.comment: Lite Carrier doesn't break out MIPI-CSI. The module itself has MIPI-CSI lanes that can be exposed by a custom carrier.

audio-jack.comment: Lite Carrier has no 3.5 mm analog jack; audio is over HDMI.

gpio-pins.comment: The Lite Carrier exposes only SIO_UART and a Gravity-4P (UART/I2C) header — no general 40-pin GPIO. The module supports up to 64 expandable GPIOs but those are reached only via custom carriers.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: No 40-pin RPi GPIO header on the Lite Carrier.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× PCIe 3.0 x4 slot + 1× M.2 M-key (PCIe 3.0 x1) + 1× M.2 E-key (PCIe 3.0 x1)
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: Lite Carrier exposes 6 PCIe 3.0 lanes total. PCIe x4 slot only powers up when using the 12V DC barrel input. The module itself has 9 PCIe 3.0 lanes available to custom carriers.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: UART (SIO_UART header) and I²C (Gravity-4P) are on the Lite Carrier; SPI is reachable through the module's edge connector but not on a labelled header.

power-input.value: DC barrel 12-20V (5.5×2.5 mm) or USB-C PD 15V; 45W minimum PSU
power-input.comment: PCIe x4 slot and the SATA function require the 12V DC barrel input (USB-C PD path supplies less). Module itself accepts DC 9-20V.

idle-power-w.comment: CNX-Software measured ~10 W at idle in the kit configuration. Snapshot from the 2024-06 review.

load-power-w.comment: Configurable TDP up to 35 W; CNX measured the cooler holding 84-85°C under sustained load with the active fan at 4000 RPM. Default mid-range (~15-20 W) draws less; this is the upper bound.

cooling-required.comment: Vendor-supplied passive heatsink hits ~91°C under Cinebench R23; vendor recommends and ships the active cooler in the evaluation kit. Passive only viable below ~10W TDP cap.

rtc-battery-header.comment: CR1220 RTC battery socket on the Lite Carrier; module also documents a 3V RTC battery input.

length-mm.comment: Lite Carrier dimensions are 146 × 102 mm (3.5-inch embedded form factor). The module alone is 69.6 × 60 mm.

form-factor-class.comment: Lite Carrier is a 3.5-inch embedded SBC outline (146×102 mm). Doesn't match Pi-standard, Pi Zero, Pico-ITX, or Nano-ITX. The bare module is a SO-DIMM-shaped SoM.

weight-g.comment: Vendor publishes the active cooler's weight (69 g) but not the bare module nor Lite Carrier weights. No reliable independent measurement found.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Standard x86-64 platform; everything is supported by mainline drivers (i915 for UHD Graphics, RTL8111 for Ethernet, AHCI/NVMe). Vendor's compatibility table marks Ubuntu 22.04/24.04, Debian 12, Fedora 39, Arch, Manjaro, OpenWRT 23.05, Proxmox VE 8.1, TrueNAS SCALE 23.10 all green.

official-os-image.comment: Vendor documentation officially supports stock Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (HWE) and 24.04 LTS images — no custom image needed; the board is a generic UEFI x86-64 system.

distros-with-images.comment: Listed only distros that are both explicitly green in LattePanda's compatibility table AND present in the schema's allowed tag set. Rocky Linux, Linux Mint, Pop!_OS, deepin, Arch, Proxmox, OpenWRT, and TrueNAS SCALE are also confirmed working via stock generic x86-64 images but have no matching tag id.

bootloader.comment: Ships with stock UEFI (AMI). Coreboot upstream gained LattePanda Mu mainboard support, so coreboot is achievable for tinkerers but isn't the factory boot path.

long-term-support.comment: No vendor-stated software-support window. Because the platform is generic UEFI x86-64, longevity is effectively governed by mainline Linux/Windows lifecycles rather than LattePanda's commitment.

android-support.comment: No official Android image; Android-x86 might work but is not vendor-supported.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Module-only price for N100 / 8GB / 64GB at DFRobot, 2026-04-26. (Launch early-bird was $99 in April 2024; current MSRP is $159.)

msrp-top-usd.comment: N305 / 16GB / 64GB module variant. Note the Mu Evaluation Kit (N100 module + Lite Carrier + active cooler) sits at $209.

available-regions.comment: Sold worldwide via DFRobot, Amazon US, Mouser/DigiKey, plus regional distributors in Europe (Botland) and Asia (Youyeetoo).

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at DFRobot and on Amazon US as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive vendor docs portal, OS compatibility matrix, design guides, plus open-source carrier KiCAD files and pinout spreadsheets. Falls short of 5 because the module's own PCB sources aren't published and a few datasheet sections are thin (electrical timing, full SI/EMI guidelines).

community-size.comment: Active vendor forum and ~2,200-member Discord, but the LattePanda audience is a fraction of RPi/Orange Pi/Rockchip ecosystems — small per RESEARCH.md thresholds.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum and Discord; recent threads on Mu carrier-board designs in 2026.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Vendor sells dedicated Mu accessories (Lite carrier, Full Eval carrier, active cooler, passive heatsink) and the design challenge has produced several community carriers (MOKA, Nano-ITX). Lacks the depth of cases/HATs/screens found in mainstream SBC ecosystems.
